title: #startdefine
breadcrumbs: ProcScript: Precompiler Directives and Uniface Constants
text: #startdefine Declares the scope of precompiler constant declarations ( #define 's) #startdefine #define Constant { = } Expression <Your code here> #enddefine Description Use the #startdefine / #enddefine directives to explicitly define the scope of precompiler constant declarations. This is especially useful if you want to define a precompiler constant that is only applicable in a specific script module, such as a trigger, operation, or entity. It is possible to nest #startdefine / #enddefine blocks. Example: Using #startdefine In the following example: An outer #startdefine block sets the scope the DEF1 declaration to two operations, myOper01 and myOper02 . An inner #startdefine block sets the scope of the def2 declaration to the myOper02 operation. Explain this code ;Script container #startdefine 1 #define DEF1 = value1 operation myOper01 ... if (something = <DEF1>) ... endif end #startdefine 2 #define DEF2 =value2 operation myOper02 ... if (something = <DEF2>) ... endif end #enddefine 2 #enddefine 1 Copy Was this topic helpful?
